skill.name: iso-certification
skill.description: A toolkit for preparing ISO 13485:2016 certification documentation for medical device QMS. Use when you need to perform a documentation gap analysis, draft or update a Quality Manual, create required procedures/work instructions, build Medical Device Files (MDF), interpret ISO 13485 clauses, or identify missing documents for certification (often triggered by ISO 13485, QMS certification, FDA QMSR, EU MDR, or quality system documentation requests).
skill.summary: Generate ISO 13485:2016 QMS documentation with templates, gap analysis, and clause-by-clause guidance for medical device certification.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 373 potential issues (risk score 100/100) but all findings are false positives when evaluated in context. The "weak cryptographic algorithm" findings are ISO 13485 clause numbers (e.g., 7.5.9.2) and document section headers misidentified as crypto references. The "env_access" findings are template placeholders like [Name] and [Date] in markdown templates. The "external_commands" findings are shell command examples in SKILL.md code blocks. The only real capabilities are a Python gap analysis script that reads user-specified directories and writes JSON output reports.

low_findings.title: Template Placeholders Misidentified as Environment Access
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged template brackets like [Name], [Date], [Company Name], and [Location/System] in markdown templates as configuration library access. These are standard QMS document template placeholders for users to fill in during customization. No actual environment variables are accessed by the templates.

low_findings.title: ISO Clause Numbers Misidentified as Cryptographic Algorithms
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 200+ instances of ISO 13485:2016 clause numbers (e.g., 4.2.3, 7.5.9.2, 8.5.2) and document section headers as weak cryptographic algorithms. These are standard regulatory clause identifiers in QMS documentation, not cryptographic references. All findings are false positives.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Examples in Markdown Misidentified as Execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged shell commands within SKILL.md code blocks and inline backtick references as external command execution. All instances are documentation examples showing users how to run the gap analyzer or reference file paths. Lines 21-25 are list items describing features, lines 39-69 are a bash code block with example usage, and line 43 is a comment showing Windows PowerShell activation.

low_findings.title: ISO Clause Numbers Misidentified as Hardcoded IP Addresses
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged ISO clause numbers 7.5.9.1 and 7.5.9.2 in gap-analysis-checklist.md as hardcoded IP addresses. These are traceability clause identifiers in the ISO 13485 standard, not network addresses. The findings in mandatory-documents.md and iso-13485-requirements.md are similarly clause references or requirement identifiers.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Keywords in Quality Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged terms like quality system investigation, root cause analysis, and network-related process terms as system/network reconnaissance. These are standard QMS process descriptions involving investigation of nonconformities and communication processes within a quality management system context.

low_findings.title: GitHub Source URL in Skill Documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged the GitHub source URL in SKILL.md line 7 as a hardcoded URL. This is the legitimate source repository link for the skill. No network connections are initiated by this skill at runtime.

content.user_title: Prepare ISO 13485 Certification Documents for Medical Device QMS
content.value_statement: Organizations seeking ISO 13485:2016 certification often struggle to identify which documents are required and how to structure their Quality Management System documentation. This skill provides ready-to-use templates, clause-by-clause guidance, and automated gap analysis to streamline certification preparation.
actual_capabilities: Run automated keyword-based gap analysis against all ISO 13485:2016 required procedures
Generate Quality Manual, CAPA procedure, and document control procedure templates
Cross-reference ISO 13485 clauses with mandatory documentation requirements and records
Identify missing procedures and generate prioritized remediation recommendations
Map Medical Device File (MDF) content requirements per ISO 13485 Clause 4.2.3
Assess existing QMS documentation using a comprehensive audit-style checklist
limitations: The gap analyzer performs keyword-based document detection, not semantic understanding of content
Templates must be customized with organization-specific roles, deadlines, and regulatory context
Does not replace a certified auditor or accredited certification body assessment
The Python gap analyzer script reads plain text and markdown files; binary formats need manual review

use_cases.title: Quality Manager Preparing for Initial Certification Audit
use_cases.description: A quality manager uses the gap analyzer to scan existing QMS documents, identifies missing procedures, and generates templates to fill gaps before the certification audit.

use_cases.title: Compliance Consultant Assessing Client QMS Readiness
use_cases.description: A regulatory consultant runs the gap analysis on a client documentation set and uses the clause reference guide to build a remediation plan with prioritized action items.

use_cases.title: Medical Device Startup Building QMS from Scratch
use_cases.description: A startup founder uses the quality manual template, procedure templates, and mandatory documents reference to build a complete QMS documentation set from zero.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Gap Analysis on Existing QMS Documents
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the ISO 13485 gap analysis script on my QMS documents in [path/to/docs] and generate a report. Analyze the output to identify which procedures are missing and provide a prioritized action plan.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has existing QMS documents and wants to assess gaps against ISO 13485 requirements

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Corrective Action Procedure from Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the CAPA-procedure-template.md, create a complete corrective and preventive action procedure for a medical device company specializing in [device type]. Customize roles, timelines, and escalation criteria for a team of 50 employees.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to create a compliant CAPA procedure for their organization

prompt_templates.title: Perform a Full Certification Readiness Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the gap-analysis-checklist.md and iso-13485-requirements.md, assess my current QMS documentation against every clause. Identify high-risk gaps for an upcoming certification audit scheduled for [date].
prompt_templates.scenario: User is weeks away from a certification audit and needs a readiness assessment

prompt_templates.title: Create a Medical Device File (MDF) per Clause 4.2.3
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through creating a Medical Device File for a [device type]. Using the MDF content model in SKILL.md, structure a document that includes device description, labeling specifications, manufacturing procedures, risk management documentation, and verification evidence.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to build a compliant Medical Device File

output_examples.input: Run gap analysis on my QMS documents in ./qms-docs/
output_examples.output: The gap analyzer scanned 12 documents and found 18 of 31 required procedures covered. Missing critical procedures include Complaint Handling (8.2.2), Corrective Action (8.5.2), and Internal Audit (8.2.4). High priority: develop CAPA procedure using the provided template first, then complaint handling.

output_examples.input: I need a Quality Manual for my Class II medical device company
output_examples.output: Using the quality-manual-template.md and quality-manual-guide.md, I will help you build a manual that covers QMS scope, process interactions, documented procedures referencing, and top management commitments. Key sections include Clause 4 scope and exclusions, Clause 5 management responsibility, and the process interaction map.

best_practices: Customize all templates with organization-specific roles, responsibilities, and process details before implementation
Combine gap analysis results with internal audit findings for a complete certification readiness picture
Run the gap analyzer iteratively as you create documents to track progress toward full coverage
anti_patterns: Using templates without customizing roles, deadlines, and organization-specific regulatory context
Treating keyword-based gap analysis results as a substitute for thorough auditor review
Skipping the Quality Manual and quality policy in favor of creating only operational procedures

faq.question: What is ISO 13485 certification?
faq.answer: ISO 13485:2016 is the international standard for Quality Management Systems (QMS) for medical device manufacturers. Certification demonstrates that an organization has a compliant QMS for design, production, and distribution of medical devices.

faq.question: Does this skill replace a certification body or auditor?
faq.answer: No. This skill helps prepare documentation and identify gaps, but it cannot replace a certified auditor or accredited certification body. A certified body must perform the final audit for ISO 13485 certification.

faq.question: How do I run the gap analysis script?
faq.answer: Install Python 3.10 or later. Run: python scripts/gap_analyzer.py --docs-dir ./your-qms-docs --output ./gap-report.json. The script scans files for keywords matching required ISO 13485 procedures.

faq.question: What documents does this skill help me create?
faq.answer: It provides templates for a Quality Manual, CAPA procedure, and Document Control procedure. It also includes reference guides for all 31 mandatory procedures, a gap analysis checklist, and a Medical Device File content model.

faq.question: Can I use this for FDA QMSR compliance?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill references FDA QMSR harmonization concepts in the Medical Device File guidance and gap analysis checklist. However, you should verify that all FDA-specific requirements are addressed separately.

faq.question: What is a Medical Device File (MDF)?
faq.answer: An MDF (per ISO 13485 Clause 4.2.3) consolidates documentation for each device type, including device description, labeling, specifications, manufacturing procedures, risk management, and verification evidence. It is similar to a Device Master Record.
